title: Roegneria interrupta
intro: Roegneria interrupta is a species of flowering plant in the grass family, Poaceae, native to the mountainous regions of China. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Roegneria interrupta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal activities. In traditional Chinese medicine, Roegneria interrupta is often used to make decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory problems, skin conditions, and digestive disorders.
taxonomy: Roegneria interrupta is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Roegneria, which is a group of grasses native to Asia. Roegneria interrupta is further classified into the subfamily Pooideae and the tribe Triticeae. The species is also known as a type of wild grass found in mountainous regions. Its classification is closely related to other species of Roegneria that are found in similar habitats.
